name: Bill Stapleton
abstract: William John Stapleton (May 4, 1945 – July 14, 1984) was an American jazz trumpeter and arranger. Stapleton was born in Blue Island, Illinois, and attended North Texas State University (1963–67, 1971), where he played in the One O'Clock Lab Band.

about_quotes.speaker: Frank Tiberi
about_quotes.evidence: But I wrote that chorale while I was playing with him and of course I mean Bill Stapleton and I collaborated on that and wrote the jazztets.
about_quotes.doc_title: Frank Tiberi interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, May 30, 2022

about_quotes.speaker: Woody Herman
about_quotes.evidence: he picked two people -- two or three which had been former writers of mine... for instance... it had to be Bill Stapleton

about_quotes.speaker: Ed Soph
about_quotes.evidence: The writers that he had were so good. Alan Broadbent, Tony Klatka, and Bill Stapleton. Great charts.
about_quotes.doc_title: Ed Soph interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, February 9, 2024

about_quotes.speaker: John LaBarbera
about_quotes.evidence: Bill Stapleton, with Woody Herman’s band, he did that, he stole that from Thad too.
